title: Three independent audit-safe policy states
evidence: Each implementation owns separate module-scoped mode and override state.
Characterization transitions in one implementation are invisible to the other two.
Runtime tests independently exhaust the core and persistence-service counters and interleave updates without cross-module visibility.
observedBehavior: Modes, supported values, overrides, failure semantics, persistence, and reset behavior disagree across the three implementations.
rollbackApproach: Revert any future ownership or synchronization change independently while retaining characterization tests.

title: Audit-safe state mutates before durable logging completes
evidence: Core setter mutates then awaits audit-store insertion.
Persistence setter mutates then performs best-effort file append.
Toggle setter mutates before console logging.
observedBehavior: A caller can receive an error while the new state remains active; interleaved core updates have no mutex or rollback.
rollbackApproach: Restore current mutation-before-log ordering if a later atomicity change causes operational regressions.

title: Machine-local compiled service discovery includes source-orphaned files
evidence: Source loader evaluates 134 files.
Current dist loader evaluates 138 files.
Four compiled-only service JavaScript files have no source counterpart.
The build does not clean dist before compilation.
dist/ is ignored generated state and is not content-addressed by the source commit.
observedBehavior: The observed local compiled loader inventory is defined by current generated-directory contents, not solely by source; whether an identical directory is deployed was not established.
rollbackApproach: Restore the prior build pipeline and generated directory if cleaning changes deployed artifacts unexpectedly.

title: Loader accepts malformed definitions after evaluating every candidate
evidence: Acceptance checks only truthiness of default and default.actions.
Empty objects and malformed truthy actions values are accepted.
Rejected modules have already executed top-level code.
observedBehavior: Malformed modules can enter the result, while rejected modules can still register timers/listeners or mutate state.
rollbackApproach: Restore the permissive predicate and raw scan behavior if validation rejects an existing compatibility module.

title: Loader cache is mutable, non-coalesced, and does not reset ESM state
evidence: Repeated calls return the same mutable array.
Concurrent cold loads perform multiple directory scans.
clearModuleDefinitionCache only clears the array reference.
observedBehavior: Caller mutation affects later results; concurrent scans race; failed or side-effectful imports persist after loader reset.
rollbackApproach: Restore the array-only cache if coalescing or defensive copies alter callers.

title: Ask and GPT waiters map cancellation and expiration differently
evidence: Ask collapses cancelled into failed.
Ask treats expired as pending.
GPT preserves cancelled and expired terminal states.
observedBehavior: The same stored job lifecycle state yields different service and HTTP behavior.
rollbackApproach: Restore each private terminal predicate independently.

title: Queue waiters ignore request abort signals and rely entirely on wall-clock progress
evidence: Neither waiter accepts AbortSignal.
The shared sleep helper supports signals but receives only a delay.
No independent maximum poll count exists.
observedBehavior: Outer request timeout can reject while waiter work continues; backward/stalled clocks can extend polling.
rollbackApproach: Remove future signal threading or iteration bounds and restore current dependency signatures.

title: Database disconnection is indistinguishable from a missing job
evidence: Default repository returns null for both disconnected database and absent row.
Both waiters map every null to missing.
observedBehavior: Operational outage can be reported as job disappearance.
rollbackApproach: Revert to null-only repository results if callers cannot consume a richer error contract.

title: Worker Responses converter drops terminal metadata
evidence: Worker hardcodes finish_reason stop.
Worker omits response status, incomplete details, truncation, and content-filter metadata.
Golden fixtures reproduce differences.
observedBehavior: Truncated, filtered, incomplete, and tool-call provider outputs appear as ordinary stopped completions in workers.
rollbackApproach: Revert worker mapping independently while preserving golden fixtures.

title: Legacy converters discard refusal and tool payloads
evidence: Refusal parts produce empty content and explicit refusal null.
Core/service can emit finish_reason tool_calls without message.tool_calls.
Worker emits stop and also drops the call.
observedBehavior: Security refusal and actionable tool-call information can be lost at the compatibility boundary.
rollbackApproach: Restore characterized field omission if consumers reject richer legacy messages.

title: Multiple HTTP method/path collisions are order-dependent
evidence: Reusable router is nested in apiRouter and mounted directly.
Audit, update, and health handlers also have competing registrations.
Middleware stacks differ between registrations.
Runtime composition proves nested-first health handling executes the memory gate, while direct-first handling bypasses it.
observedBehavior: Earlier terminating handlers shadow later stacks; duplicate reusable routes have different middleware ancestry.
rollbackApproach: Restore the removed mount in its original order and reinstate its middleware chain.

title: Seven timing-safe credential comparisons have different normalization contracts
evidence: Five raw-buffer length-guard implementations and two hash-based implementations exist.
Whitespace, Bearer parsing, header precedence, case, maximum length, and configured-token trimming differ.
observedBehavior: Equivalent-looking credentials can authorize on one surface and fail on another; none performs Unicode normalization.
rollbackApproach: Revert each surface independently; do not centralize until compatibility decisions are explicit.

title: Configured 100% coverage represents a bounded root-backend and monorepo production slice
evidence: Coverage scope lists 94 unique existing files.
Executable src denominator is 717 files.
Seven explicit monorepo production roots contain 803 non-declaration TypeScript files.
Jest applies per-file 100% thresholds only to the curated list.
observedBehavior: The scoped metric represents 13.1102% of root-backend src files and 11.7061% of monorepo production-root files; it can be mistaken for repository-wide 100% without both labels.
rollbackApproach: Restore prior labels while retaining the denominator artifact if downstream dashboards depend on wording.

title: Named boundary check does not execute dependency-cycle analysis
evidence: npm run check:boundaries invokes scripts/check-boundaries.js.
That file runs the CEF scanner.
Madge still detects 63 circular paths.
observedBehavior: CI can report the named boundary check as passing while runtime dependency cycles remain unchecked.
rollbackApproach: Restore the prior command target if newly enforced cycle checks block releases unexpectedly.

title: Two runtime SCCs span 75 modules
evidence: AST graph contains 2,310 runtime edges after explicit type-only edges are removed.
SCC sizes are 67 and 8.
Cross-area imports include both core-to-services and services-to-core edges.
observedBehavior: Initialization order, import-time state, and boundary ownership are coupled across major architectural areas.
suggestedImplementationPhase: Phase 3 incremental boundary cuts after Phase 2 correctness decisions
rollbackApproach: Revert one boundary cut at a time and restore the original direct import edge.

title: Ask and service system-state builders match while request/error contracts differ
evidence: Success builders are field-for-field duplicates.
Ask sanitizes and validates more narrowly.
Invalid and conflict envelopes differ.
The shared module-scoped intent store exposes no reset function; characterization isolates inserts with unique session IDs.
observedBehavior: Successful anonymous reads can match exactly, but clients see different validation, headers, side effects, and error shapes.
rollbackApproach: Restore independent builders if delegating one surface changes route-specific contracts.

title: HTTP and MCP CLEAR recheck builders are exact duplicates
evidence: Both emit the same fields in the same order.
Both preserve actions and params without normalization.
Both use non-null rollback detection.
Actual HTTP and createMcpServer plans.execute execution produces equal recheck payloads.
observedBehavior: The same concept is implemented twice, while downstream HTTP and MCP envelopes remain different.
rollbackApproach: Restore both local builders if a shared pure function changes serialization or dependency boundaries.

title: HTTP and MCP CLEAR execution preserve divergent errors and null-score persistence
evidence: Capability rejection stops both surfaces before CLEAR evaluation or persistence, but response envelopes differ.
A blocking CLEAR decision calls blockPlan on both surfaces; HTTP returns 403 and MCP returns ERR_GATED.
An allow decision whose stored clearScore is null persists clearDecision block on both surfaces.
A blockPlan failure becomes a generic HTTP 500, while MCP ERR_INTERNAL includes the dependency-thrown message.
observedBehavior: Shared CLEAR inputs can produce equivalent persistence decisions but surface-specific error envelopes; null scores invert the persisted allow decision, and MCP can disclose a dependency error message.
suggestedImplementationPhase: Phase 2 CLEAR correctness and error-contract review
rollbackApproach: Revert null-score mapping and error-envelope changes independently while retaining the shared runtime fixture matrix.

title: Dedicated DB worker transitively starts the in-process listener runtime
evidence: The Railway launcher selects src/workers/jobRunner.ts for ARCANOS_PROCESS_KIND=worker and derives RUN_WORKERS=true.
jobRunner imports gptDispatch, which imports routes/modules.ts; that module performs a top-level loadModuleDefinitions call.
The loader evaluates workerControlService.ts even though the deterministic inventory rejects it as noDefaultExport.
workerControlService.ts imports workerConfig.ts, whose import-time branch calls startWorkers when worker mode is enabled.
The in-process runtime registers EventEmitter handlers, defaulting to four listeners, while jobRunner independently polls PostgreSQL.
observedBehavior: Repository wiring designates jobRunner as the PostgreSQL queue owner, but its import graph also starts the separate in-process listener lifecycle. The listeners do not claim database jobs; live deployed listener counts were not observed.
observationType: static plus deterministic loader inventory; live deployment not observed
requiredPrerequisiteTests: Isolated worker-entrypoint import with database, Redis, OpenAI, network, signal, timer, and listener stubs
rollbackApproach: Revert any later startup-boundary change, rebuild and redeploy only the worker service, then verify readiness, bootstrap logs, queue claims, and absence of duplicate completion events.
